resources.string: Ask anything — answers cite your local notes.
Plain-language answers grounded in your notes
Editable note drafts with review cards included
Interview-style quizzing that becomes review cards
Review mode · name a topic and I’ll quiz you
Review mode · answer and I’ll evaluate
Draft mode · send to keep improving the draft
Set up an AI service to continue this conversation.
Working draft updated. Open it to review or edit.
What would you like to review today? Name an Area, course, note, or interview topic and I will ask one question.
No. Recheck the topics and propose a better selection.
I can turn this into an editable node draft first. You can review it before saving.
Build one editable Markdown note first.
Include app-readable :::quiz review cards in the note.
Preserve unrelated leftovers as follow-up capture suggestions.
No. Ask me clarifying questions or propose a safer split first.
Use the assistant as an optional, supervised study helper. It can explain concepts, prepare editable drafts, and cite local matches, but every final save still happens only after your review.
What the assistant can do, how to use it safely, and how to recover from mistakes.
What it can help with now
Draft ready. Review and save it when you agree.
Draft ready. Choose an Area before saving so its review cards appear in Review.
Could not save this reply. Try again.
That local source is no longer available.

string-array.name: assistant_guide_capabilities
string-array.item: Explain concepts, summarize notes, and answer study questions with your configured AI provider plus short local search excerpts.
Prepare editable Markdown drafts from requests such as creating a note, outlining a topic, or reorganizing a Capture slip.
Suggest revisions for an existing Node, Quiz, or Capture slip while keeping local object identity and revision checks intact.
Support review planning and question-driven study help, including guided follow-up in the same conversation window.
Show citations for local matches so you can inspect the basis of a reply instead of trusting an unsupported answer.
Ask for lightweight confirmation when a drafting or save-related step needs your decision.

string-array.name: assistant_guide_workflow
string-array.item: First save and validate AI settings under More - Service. If validation fails, fix the base URL, model, or API key before sending assistant requests.
Ask for one concrete task at a time: explain a concept, turn a Capture slip into a draft, or help plan a review session.
Treat every generated answer or draft as a proposal. Review the title, Area, Markdown body, and quiz cards before saving anything.
Keep follow-up questions in the same assistant window when they belong to one topic; start a new chat when you switch tasks.
Store large source material in local Nodes or Capture slips instead of pasting everything into chat, because only short local excerpts are sent for retrieval.

string-array.name: assistant_guide_safety
string-array.item: AI is optional. Notes, Capture, Review, Trash, and backups continue to work locally even if no provider is configured.
Only HTTPS provider endpoints are accepted, and API keys stay on this device protected by Android Keystore.
The assistant cannot silently create, overwrite, move, or delete local Nodes and Areas. Draft, capture-save, and review-save still require an explicit app action.
Unknown Area names are not treated as real Areas, and revision checks prevent stale drafts from silently replacing newer local edits.
Do not send secrets or material you would not want shared with the external AI provider you configured.

string-array.name: assistant_guide_rollback
string-array.item: If a reply or draft is wrong, ask for a revision or leave the editor without saving. No silent write happens until you confirm Save.
If you saved a bad Node, edit it manually or move it to Trash. Trash can restore it until you choose Delete forever.
Before cleanup, restore, or device changes, export a backup from More - Data. Restore replaces current local data instead of merging it.
If assistant requests stop working, return to More - Service and run Validate or Models before retrying.

resources.string: Local Markdown, Room database, explicit JSON backup. No account required; AI is optional.
Choose a card to inspect details, edit Markdown, add quiz cards, and keep your local study loop moving.
Structure stays %1$s. Overview and map stay collapsed so you can reach nodes faster.
Folders come first on mobile. Overview stays available, but it should not block the file browser flow.
Tap an area to collapse or expand. This mirrors the desktop knowledge map as a phone-friendly outline.
This is now a lightweight folder map: quick counts, light previews, no giant cards before the main list.
Create a Markdown note. The phone app works without desktop, account, network, or AI.
%1$d nodes hidden. Open this area when you want to drill into tracks.
No body yet. Add Markdown to make this node useful.
The Library now starts as a folder list. Create an Area, then put nodes inside it.
Create a node here, or move one in from another area.
No checked nodes in this area yet.
Open a node from the library first.
Save questions while reading. They stay unresolved until you mark them resolved, export them, or fold them into Markdown and quiz edits.
Example: This explanation skips why %%eax changes here.
Write or trim Markdown here. :::quiz blocks still work.
Search local nodes and quiz cards on this device.
Type a query and search. Results open directly into reader or review.
Linked to %1$s. Add the prompt and answer, then save.
%1$d due now / %2$d total cards. Reveal the answer, then rate it.
No review cards in this Area yet.
Add quiz cards from a note or Markdown :::quiz block. Due cards will appear here.
Answer is hidden until you reveal it.
Answer from memory first, then reveal the answer.
This card was saved and moved into its next review step.
Read the full explanation, then choose what to review next.
Choose an Area, then start its due review queue.
Export first. Restore is a full replacement: it deletes current local data and replaces it with the selected backup.
Exports use a .txt filename with JSON inside so QQ, WeChat, and file managers share it more reliably on Android. Import reads .txt or .json, then restore replaces current local data.
A restore replaces current local data on this phone, so export first if there is anything here you may need later.
Search local knowledge, capture Markdown, or jump into due review from one offline command center.
Create or open a node. Your next visit will surface the most recent learning thread here.
No nodes yet. Create the first Markdown node from the action card above.
%1$d review cards due today. Keep the streak going.
All caught up. Capture something new today.
Ask questions, draft notes, and review with quizzes.
Create a Markdown node and it will show up here next time.
Capture the smallest useful fragment first, then decide whether it becomes Markdown, a quiz, or an AI draft.
Example: I do not understand why TLB miss triggers a page table walk.
See what the optional AI path sends and when it actually creates a draft.
AI chain: save slip -> review preflight -> Validate if needed -> Generate -> edit Markdown -> Save. The model never creates a final node without your Save Markdown.
Ready to generate an editable node draft
Target: %1$s / Source: %2$s. The result opens in Edit Mode and still needs your Save Markdown confirmation.
No slips yet. The goal is not long writing; it is catching useful fragments before they vanish.
No archived slips yet. Archive from Inbox when you want to hide a slip without deleting it.
The model is working on this slip. The result will open as an editable Markdown draft before any node is saved.
Ready to generate an editable Markdown draft. The preflight card shows every slip field and node-title context that will be sent.
A draft was generated. Review and save it from Edit Mode, or generate again if you left the editor.
Save the slip locally now. Configure a provider later if you want AI to expand it into Markdown.
Promote this slip manually into a Markdown node, or queue it for AI drafting.
This slip is saved locally. Turn it into a node, improve it with AI, or archive it.
This slip is archived locally. Restore it to bring it back to Inbox, or delete it forever.
This slip still carries a prior AI %1$s state from an older workflow. Use the single Improve with AI action to continue; no old preflight path will run.
Delete archived slip “%1$s” forever? This cannot be undone in the app and is only recoverable from a backup.
System, AI, notifications, and data tools stay collapsed until you need them.
Language and day and night display.
Optional AI provider for capture drafting.
Pull a phone-friendly study subset from desktop: review cards, Daily Bite cards, light reading nodes, captures, and review progress. Complex knowledge graph structure stays desktop-first.
Lightweight review cards synced from desktop. Tap to flip and rate your recall.
No bite cards yet. Extract cards from quizzes on your desktop and sync them here.
Open CS Learning OS on your computer, go to Sync, and click “Generate pairing token”. Then paste the endpoint and token below.
1. Keep this app open and make sure your phone is on the same Wi-Fi network as your computer (or reachable via Tailscale).
2. On your computer, open CS Learning OS and click “Sync” in the left sidebar.
3. Click “Generate pairing token”. The token is valid for 10 minutes and can only be created on the desktop itself.
4. Copy the Endpoint and Token shown on the desktop, then paste them into the fields below and tap Pair.
Tip: start the desktop with .\scripts\dev.ps1 -EnableLanSync. The desktop will display a Wi-Fi address such as http://192.168.1.x:8001; do not use 127.0.0.1 on your phone.
Desktop paired. Pull to download your phone-friendly study subset.
Study pull complete: %1$d phone-ready updates, %2$d conflicts.
Cannot reach the desktop. Check the endpoint and network.
Pairing failed: the token is invalid or expired.
The desktop changed this device sync permission. Check Sync on your computer.
The desktop rejected the sync request.
Last pull: %1$d reading nodes, %2$d quizzes, %3$d bites, %4$d conflicts
Add %1$d · Update %2$d · Skip conflicts %3$d · Skip invalid %4$d
Conflicting local edits are never overwritten; they stay untouched and count as skipped conflicts.
The package is invalid or corrupted.
The package is too large to import.
This package version is not supported.
Built-in instructions for the current local-first phone workflow.
No notifications yet. Capture and AI draft progress will appear here.
Saved on this phone. Follow system uses the phone language when the app has matching copy.
Follow system tracks the Android display setting. Day and Night override it locally.
Auto-saved on this phone. Save confirms the current fields; Validate tests /models; Models pulls selectable model IDs.
Export first before restore or cleanup. Restore is a full replacement; Trash is reversible until you choose Delete forever.
No trashed nodes. Deleting from Reader moves a node here first, so Trash stays reversible.
Trash is reversible. Delete forever is only recoverable from a backup.
This removes the built-in starter demo nodes from this phone. Export first if you edited demo content and want to keep it.
Delete “%1$s” forever? This cannot be undone in the app and is only recoverable from a backup.

This app is local first: study content, notes, capture slips, review history, and backups stay on this device by default. Use this guide to configure optional AI, turn a Capture slip into a reviewed note, and protect your data before restore or device changes.
Works offline with no account, desktop, or API key. AI is optional; your notes, review cards, Trash, and backups stay local.

string-array.name: more_guide_step_titles
string-array.item: Confirm the local-first basics
Configure AI only if you need it
Turn Capture into a reviewed note
Back up before restore or cleanup

string-array.name: more_guide_step_bodies
string-array.item: Home, Capture, Library, Review, and More all work from local Room data. You do not need an account, desktop app, or server for the core study flow.
Open More - Service, save provider name, API key, HTTPS base URL, and model, then run Validate. AI is optional and generated output is always a proposal.
Create or open a Capture slip, generate an AI draft if configured, review the title, Area, Markdown, and quiz cards, then save only when the note is ready for Library.
Use More - Data to export a backup before restore, deleting forever, local cleanup, or moving to another phone. Restore replaces the current local dataset.

resources.string: Add a title or some Markdown before saving.
Choose an Area before saving this new node.
Write the unclear point before saving it.
Question saved to unresolved reader queue
Write a quick note before saving the capture slip.
Write a quick note before saving it for AI drafting.
Slip saved. Review AI draft preflight.
Review this capture draft before saving it as Markdown.
AI draft ready. Review before saving.
Choose an Area before saving this standalone review card.
Added to near-term practice. Review the explanation once, then try it again.
Marked as a weak point. This card will return sooner for focused practice.
Good work. This card moved forward in your review queue.
Move or remove the nodes in this area before deleting it
Node moved to the selected area
That backup file is not valid JSON.
Readable Markdown and TXT export generated
Could not read that backup file.
This device could not securely save the AI settings.
Add a provider, API key, base URL, and model to turn capture slips into editable Markdown drafts.
Settings are saved automatically on this phone. Tap Save settings for confirmation, then Validate or Models.
Slip saved. Confirm the target, optionally validate the provider, then generate an editable Markdown node draft.
The slip is waiting in Capture. Review what will be sent, then tap Generate draft.
The slip is safe locally. Configure Service before using AI draft.
Add %1$s before generating a capture draft.
This will send the capture slip plus the shown node-title context to %1$s. The result opens as an editable Markdown draft; nothing becomes a node until Save Markdown.
Review the preflight card, then generate an editable Markdown draft.
Sending this slip to your configured OpenAI-compatible model. The result will open as an editable Markdown node draft.
The model is expanding one capture slip into an editable Markdown draft.
Review the Markdown, edit anything suspicious, then Save Markdown to convert the slip into a node.
A Markdown draft opened in Edit Mode. Save Markdown when you approve it.
Calling %1$s with your saved API key.
The endpoint responded, but did not return model IDs.
Provider, API key, base URL, model, and thinking preference are saved locally on this phone.
Calling %1$s. If your provider blocks model listing, type the model manually.
The endpoint responded but returned no IDs. You can still type a model manually.
Tap a model below to use it for Capture Slip AI drafts.
Settings are stored locally as you type. Validate tests the provider before Capture sends a slip.
